prompt_text: Open source or affordable product feedback tools instead of Aha!
response:
You have two main options instead of Aha!: **open source, self‑hosted tools** (often free except hosting) and **affordable SaaS products** with generous free/low‑cost tiers.

Below are the strongest options in each category.

---

## Open source product feedback tools (Aha-style)

These give you idea boards, voting, sometimes roadmap + changelog, and you own the data.

**1. Fider** – simple, battle‑tested feedback board  
- **What it is:** Open source feedback board with idea submission, voting, comments, status updates.[1][3]  
- **Why it’s good:**  
  - Very common choice; actively maintained and widely used.[1][3]  
  - Self‑host with Docker; can run on very small servers (e.g. ~$5/month VPS).[1]  
  - Great if you just need **feature voting + basic status**.  
- **Trade‑offs:**  
  - No built‑in roadmap/changelog or deep integrations; more “Canny‑style board” than full Aha replacement.[3]

**2. ClearFlask** – open‑source feedback + roadmap + announcements  
- **What it is:** Open‑source “ideation tool” that includes **feedback, public roadmap, and announcements**.[6][3]  
- **Deployment:**  
  - Open source, **no limitations when self‑hosted**; you own the data on your infra.[6]  
  - Also has a cloud version with “pay for what you use” pricing.[6]  
- **Why it’s good:**  
  - Closer to Aha workflow: collect ideas → prioritize → show roadmap → publish announcements.  
- **Trade‑offs:**  
  - More complex to configure than Fider; you’ll want someone comfortable with Docker and basic ops.[3]

**3. Quackback** – full workflow, heavy on integrations  
- **What it is:** Open source customer feedback platform with **boards, roadmap, changelog, SSO/OIDC, REST API**, and **23+ integrations** (Slack, Linear, Jira, GitHub, Intercom, Zendesk, Salesforce, etc.).[3]  
- **Why it’s good:**  
  - Designed as a **full Aha!/Canny‑style suite**: voting + roadmap + changelog + integrations.[3]  
  - Good pick if you need enterprise‑ish features (SSO, multiple tools integration) but want open source.  
- **Trade‑offs:**  
  - More moving parts; best suited to teams that can maintain a more complex self‑hosted app.

**4. Astuto** – lightweight open source feedback board  
- **What it is:** Free, open‑source, self‑hosted feedback tool to collect, manage and prioritize customer feedback.[4][3]  
- **Why it’s good:**  
  - Simple UI; supports idea submission, voting, comments.[3][4]  
  - Rails + React; deployable with Docker.[3]  
- **Trade‑offs:**  
  - Fewer features than Quackback / ClearFlask; mainly a feedback board rather than full product ops.

**5. LogChimp** – open source feedback + roadmap  
- **What it is:** Node.js/Vue‑based open source feedback and roadmap tool.[3]  
- **Why it’s good:**  
  - Good if you prefer JS stack and mainly need board + roadmap.  
- **Trade‑offs:**  
  - Smaller ecosystem; you may do more configuration yourself.[3]

**When to choose open source:**  
- You want to **own your data** and avoid per‑seat/per‑MAU fees.  
- You have someone who can manage Docker / a VPS.  
- You’re OK trading some polish and “batteries included” for control and cost.

If you want the **closest open‑source Aha analogue**, look at **Quackback or ClearFlask** first, then Fider if you only need simple boards.

---

## Affordable / “actually free” SaaS feedback tools

If you prefer hosted tools but want to avoid Aha pricing, these are strong lower‑cost options.

**1. UserJot** – very generous free plan  
- **What it is:** Modern product feedback tool with idea boards.  
- **Pricing:**  
  - Free plan with **unlimited users and posts**, up to **2 boards**, free forever, no credit card.[1]  
- **Why it’s good:**  
  - For most teams, 2 boards (e.g., “Feature Requests”, “Bugs”) is enough initially.[1]  
  - The article explicitly calls it “the best free alternative” for ongoing feedback collection.[1]
